nodes.name: Inventions
nodes.description: Great inventions and the people behind them

items.prompt: Who is credited with patenting the telephone in 1876?
items.explanation:
Received U.S. patent No. 174,465 on March 7, 1876, for 'an apparatus for transmitting vocal sounds'
First intelligible telephone call: 'Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.' (March 10, 1876)
Elisha Gray filed a patent caveat for a similar device the same day; priority dispute lasted years
Born in Edinburgh, Scotland; emigrated to Canada, then the U.S.
Also worked on the photophone (transmitted sound via light) and early metal detectors
Co-founded the National Geographic Society in 1888
Antonio Meucci is recognized by the U.S. Congress (2002) for earlier work on voice communication devices

items.prompt: Who developed the first commercially practical incandescent light bulb in 1879?
items.explanation:
Demonstrated a carbon-filament bulb lasting 13.5 hours on October 21, 1879, at Menlo Park, NJ
By 1880, improved bamboo filaments lasted over 1,200 hours
Edison did not 'invent' the light bulb from scratch; he improved on decades of prior work by Humphry Davy, Warren de la Rue, and Joseph Swan
Established the first commercial power station, Pearl Street Station, in Manhattan (1882)
Holds 1,093 U.S. patents, still the fourth-most prolific inventor in patent history
Menlo Park earned him the nickname 'The Wizard of Menlo Park'
Famous rivalry with Nikola Tesla over AC vs. DC current ('War of Currents')

items.prompt: Who invented the movable-type printing press in Europe around 1440?
items.explanation:
Developed the movable-type printing press in Mainz, Germany, around 1440
Key innovation was a hand mould for rapidly casting individual metal type pieces
The Gutenberg Bible (~1455) was the first major book printed with movable type in the West
A single press could produce 3,600 pages per workday vs. 40 by hand-printing
Gutenberg was a goldsmith by trade; his metalworking skills were essential to the invention
Lost his press to investor Johann Fust in a lawsuit over unpaid debts
Chinese inventor Bi Sheng created movable type in the 11th century, but Gutenberg's system was independently developed and far more widely adopted

items.prompt: Who invented the World Wide Web while working at CERN in 1989?
items.explanation:
Proposed the Web in a March 1989 paper titled 'Information Management: A Proposal'
Built the first web browser, server, and website by December 1990 at CERN in Geneva
The first website described the World Wide Web project itself
Invented HTML, HTTP, and URLs as the three pillars of the Web
CERN released the Web software into the public domain on April 30, 1993, enabling its explosion
Knighted by Queen Elizabeth II in 2004; holds the Order of Merit
British computer scientist, born in London; both parents worked on the Ferranti Mark 1 computer

items.prompt: Which brothers achieved the first sustained, controlled, powered heavier-than-air flight on December 17, 1903?
items.explanation:
First flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, December 17, 1903; Orville piloted
The first flight lasted 12 seconds and covered 120 feet; fourth flight that day went 852 feet in 59 seconds
Owned a bicycle shop in Dayton, Ohio, which funded their aeronautical research
Neither brother had a college degree or a pilot's license
Built their own wind tunnel to test over 200 wing designs
Wilbur died of typhoid in 1912 at age 45; Orville lived until 1948
Their claim was contested by supporters of Samuel Langley, whose Aerodrome crashed into the Potomac nine days earlier

items.prompt: Which Italian inventor transmitted the first radio signals over a distance of 1.5 miles in 1895 and won the Nobel Prize in 1909?
items.explanation:
Transmitted radio signals ~1.5 miles in 1895 near Bologna, Italy
Shared the 1909 Nobel Prize in Physics with Ferdinand Braun for wireless telegraphy
Sent the first transatlantic radio signal from Cornwall, England, to Newfoundland in December 1901
The U.S. Supreme Court (1943) invalidated Marconi's radio patent, restoring prior patents by Tesla, Lodge, and Stone
Nikola Tesla demonstrated radio principles earlier but a lab fire in 1895 destroyed his work
Marconi's mother was Annie Jameson, heiress to the Jameson Irish whiskey family
Heinrich Hertz proved the existence of radio waves in 1887, building on James Clerk Maxwell's theory

items.prompt: Which American inventor demonstrated the first fully electronic television system on September 7, 1927, in San Francisco?
items.explanation:
Demonstrated the first all-electronic TV image on September 7, 1927, in San Francisco
His image dissector camera tube transmitted a simple straight line as its first image
Conceived the basic idea at age 14 while plowing a potato field in Idaho
Scottish inventor John Logie Baird demonstrated a mechanical television on January 26, 1926
Farnsworth fought a bitter patent war with RCA's Vladimir Zworykin; Farnsworth ultimately prevailed
By 1929 his system had no mechanical parts, unlike Baird's spinning-disc approach
Died in 1971 at 64; largely uncelebrated during his lifetime compared to corporate rivals

items.prompt: Who discovered penicillin in 1928 after noticing mold killing bacteria in a petri dish?
items.explanation:
Discovered penicillin on September 28, 1928, at St. Mary's Hospital, London
Found that Penicillium notatum mold inhibited Staphylococcus bacteria on a petri dish left out during vacation
Shared the 1945 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Howard Florey and Ernst Chain
Florey and Chain developed penicillin into a usable drug; mass production began during WWII
Penicillin is estimated to have saved over 200 million lives since its introduction
Fleming warned about antibiotic resistance in his 1945 Nobel lecture
Scottish bacteriologist; served in the Royal Army Medical Corps in World War I

items.prompt: Who radically improved the steam engine in 1765 by adding a separate condenser, powering the Industrial Revolution?
items.explanation:
Conceived the separate condenser in 1765 while repairing a model Newcomen engine at the University of Glasgow
The separate condenser eliminated the massive energy waste of Newcomen's design
Thomas Newcomen built the first practical steam engine in 1712 for pumping water from mines
Watt's engine was commercialized through a partnership with Matthew Boulton starting in 1775
The unit of power 'watt' is named after him; he also coined 'horsepower' as a marketing term
Scottish inventor and mechanical engineer, born in Greenock
His patent expired in 1800, unleashing a wave of steam innovation including Trevithick's locomotive

items.prompt: Who invented dynamite in 1867 and used his fortune to establish a famous prize?
items.explanation:
Patented dynamite in 1867 after discovering that nitroglycerin absorbed in diatomaceous earth was stable and safe to handle
Dynamite made blasting for construction and mining far safer than raw nitroglycerin
A premature obituary in 1888 called him a 'merchant of death,' reportedly motivating the Nobel Prizes
His will endowed the Nobel Prizes (first awarded 1901) in physics, chemistry, medicine, literature, and peace
Swedish chemist, engineer, and industrialist; held 355 patents
Owned Bofors, a major arms manufacturer, which he redirected toward steel and chemical production
Died December 10, 1896, in San Remo, Italy; Nobel Prizes are awarded on the anniversary of his death

items.prompt: Which French inventor publicly announced the daguerreotype photographic process in 1839?
items.explanation:
The daguerreotype was publicly announced on August 19, 1839, as a gift 'free to the world' by the French government
Daguerre's process required only minutes of exposure vs. hours for earlier methods
Joseph Nicéphore Niépce took the first photograph (heliograph) around 1826–1827 with an 8-hour exposure
Niépce and Daguerre became partners in 1829; Niépce died in 1833 before the process was perfected
Daguerre was originally a painter and theatrical set designer known for the Diorama in Paris
The daguerreotype produced a mirror-like image on a silver-coated copper plate
William Henry Fox Talbot independently invented the calotype (negative-positive) process in England

items.prompt: Which three physicists at Bell Labs demonstrated the first transistor on December 23, 1947?
items.explanation:
First working transistor demonstrated December 23, 1947, at Bell Labs in Murray Hill, NJ
Invented by John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, and William Shockley
The point-contact transistor used a germanium crystal with two gold contacts
Named 'transistor' by Bell Labs engineer John Pierce (trans + resistor)
All three shared the 1956 Nobel Prize in Physics
Bardeen is the only person to win two Nobel Prizes in Physics (also 1972, for superconductivity theory)
Shockley later founded Shockley Semiconductor in Palo Alto, seeding Silicon Valley; his employees left to start Fairchild Semiconductor

items.prompt: Who performed the first successful vaccination in 1796, inoculating a boy with cowpox to protect against smallpox?
items.explanation:
Inoculated 8-year-old James Phipps with cowpox on May 14, 1796, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire
Material came from a cowpox sore on milkmaid Sarah Nelmes's hand
When later exposed to smallpox, Phipps showed no sign of disease
Published results in 1798: An Inquiry into the Causes and Effects of the Variolae Vaccinae
The word 'vaccine' derives from Latin vacca ('cow'), a reference to Jenner's cowpox method
Smallpox was declared eradicated by the WHO in 1980, the only human disease eliminated by vaccination
English country doctor; often called 'the father of immunology'

items.prompt: Who invented the cotton gin in 1793, transforming Southern agriculture?
items.explanation:
Built the cotton gin in 1793 on a Georgia plantation; patented March 14, 1794
The machine separated cotton fibers from seeds, cleaning 50 lbs of cotton per day vs. 1 lb by hand
U.S. cotton exports exploded from ~500,000 lbs (1793) to 93 million lbs (1810)
Paradoxically strengthened slavery by making cotton enormously profitable in the Deep South
Whitney's patent was widely pirated; he earned almost nothing from the gin itself
Later pioneered interchangeable parts in musket manufacturing for the U.S. government
Yale graduate; invented the cotton gin within months of arriving in the South

items.prompt: Who developed the electric telegraph and sent the first message, 'What hath God wrought,' on May 24, 1844?
items.explanation:
Sent the first telegraph message from Washington, D.C., to Baltimore on May 24, 1844
The message 'What hath God wrought' (Numbers 23:23) was sent from the U.S. Capitol
Alfred Vail received the message in Baltimore and returned it successfully
Co-developed Morse Code with Vail in 1838; dots and dashes represented letters
Morse was originally a successful portrait painter before turning to invention
Congress funded the Baltimore–Washington line with a $30,000 appropriation in 1843
The telegraph revolutionized communication and enabled real-time news reporting and stock trading
